title: cistus × clausonii nothosubsp. clausonii
intro: Cistus × clausonii nothosubsp. clausonii, commonly known as the Portuguese rockrose or Clauson's cistus, is a hybrid plant species within the Cistus genus. It is a flowering shrub native to the western Mediterranean region, characterized by its white or pink flowers and dark green leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Cistus × clausonii nothosubsp. clausonii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
